sources: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation Trust (Endowment Payout)
Reinvested Returns from Breakthrough Energy and TerraPower
Direct Philanthropic Contributions by Bill Gates

maternal_child_health.name: Maternal, Newborn, and Child Health Innovation

2026.methodology: Figures are based on the Board-endorsed 2026 Budget Statement (Jan 2026) and the CEO's 'Roadmap to 2045'. Income is matched to the steady-state payout target of $9 billion. Spending breakdown reflects the 70% combined mandate for Global Health and Maternal Health goals. The Operating Expenditure is strictly capped at $1.25 billion as per the new stewardship policy to ensure maximum programmatic flow-through.

sources: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation Trust Endowment Distributions

2025.methodology: The data is based on the official 2025 Annual Budget Announcement by the Gates Foundation Board. The 'total income' for operational purposes is aligned with the approved 'budget payout' of $8.74 billion, as the foundation operates as a pass-through entity for the Foundation Trust. Spending percentages are derived from the 2025 strategic allocation goals which prioritize Global Health (aiming for 70% combined with Global Development) while capping operational costs near 13-14% to maximize programmatic impact.

sources: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation Trust Grants
Direct Contributions from Bill Gates and Melinda French Gates

other_programs.name: US Program and Global Policy Advocacy

2024.methodology: Data extracted from the 2024 Audited Consolidated Financial Statements and the 2024 Annual Report. Total income is calculated as the sum of cash received from the Trust ($8,096,000,000), direct contributions ($107,608,000), and other cash received ($28,587,000). Spending total reflects the combined charitable support ($8,010,000,000) and operational administrative adjustments identified in the fiscal year report.
